behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler↔signature-check↔queue handoff, not just hash math. A Unit test of HMAC verification alone can miss accidental enqueue-on-failure. Integration is sufficient because this behavior does not require real network/OS/subprocess, does not depend on AI/ML output, and has no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires HTTP status + no forward, not real infrastructure timing/load effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real signature middleware and a test double queue adapter that records writes. Send a request with an invalid `X-Signature-256` over the in-process app interface; assert `401` and zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). A Unit test of one function can miss cross-module ordering bugs. Integration with the real queue module and local persistence catches real failures without requiring external OS/network/subprocess. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real external HTTP/network conditions, only write-before-ack ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server + real persistent queue implementation backed by test local storage. Instrument queue `enqueue` completion and response send time; assert enqueue commit completes before `200` is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction between request path and async downstream worker trigger. Unit logic checks can miss accidental `await` on processing promises. Integration is enough: no required real network/OS primitive, no AI output dependence, no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System: behavior does not require real external infrastructure, only async decoupling semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor in-process. Make downstream processor block on a controllable promise; send request and assert response is returned before releasing processor block.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency pressure, which is sensitive to real runtime scheduling and HTTP serving behavior. In-process fakes/timers can hide queue contention/event-loop starvation issues. No AI dependency and no multi-agentic pipeline, so System (not Agentic/Workflow). Rejected Integration: text requires real-time SLA under concurrent slow prior work, which mocked timing often fails to expose.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP service process, preload slow concurrent downstream work, then issue real HTTP requests and measure end-to-end response time with wall clock. Assert each response returns within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: This is dedup state handoff across request handling and queue persistence. A Unit test of an `isDuplicate(id)` helper can miss real write-path races/integration mistakes. Integration with real dedup storage path is sufficient; no real external OS/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text, and no AI dependency exists. Rejected System: no explicit requirement for real network or OS-level phenomena.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler with real dedup/queue storage modules. Send two requests with same event ID and valid signatures; assert first enqueue succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests contract mapping between queue backpressure signal and HTTP response semantics. Unit tests of error-mapping helpers can miss wiring mistakes in handler path. Integration is enough since failure does not inherently require real OS/network/subprocess, AI output, or multi-agentic sequencing. Rejected System: behavior text is about translating a queue-full condition, not real infrastructure saturation dynamics.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler in-process with queue module configured/test-doubled to return `QUEUE_FULL`. Send valid request and assert `503` plus `Retry-After` header and no successful enqueue.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is inside queue module + its persistence layer (stateful storage interaction), not pure single-function logic. Requires real queue implementation with local persistence to catch capacity accounting bugs. Not System because this can be validated with local DB/filesystem in-process; not Agentic/Workflow because no model output decisions are involved. Rejected higher level: behavior text focuses on queue capacity enforcement, not external network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with a small configured capacity using test local storage. Enqueue until limit, then attempt one more and assert explicit rejection and unchanged stored count.
